AMD.reasoning: Three agents are directionally bullish (trend_momentum, breakout, catalyst_news), one is bearish (mean_reversion), and one is neutral (macro_context). On a raw vote count that looks like a 3-1-1 bullish tilt, but the vote count obscures critical structural disagreement about TIMING — not direction. The core split is: catalyst_news argues for entering NOW ahead of May 5 earnings (4 trading days away); trend_momentum and macro_context both explicitly state the entry is PREMATURE at current levels (~337-345) and want a pullback to the 308-315 zone before acting. That 2-agent consensus on 'wait for pullback' is more informative than the 3-1 directional vote. Breakout contributes the weakest signal (confidence 42, no daily volume confirmation, squeeze already fired at daily level) and is effectively a conditional/pending setup, not a live entry. Mean reversion fires its fade-the-extreme branch with moderate conviction (62) but acknowledges no confirmed daily reversal candle — it is a valid counterpoint, not a high-conviction short. The macro context applies its regime check and finds R/R asymmetry deeply unfavorable at current price: best-case immediate target (352.99) offers only ~2.3% upside vs ~4.7% downside to nearest hourly support (328.81), a 0.5:1 ratio far below the 2:1 minimum threshold. Even extending to Fib 1.272 at 366.67 yields only 1.3:1. Per the system mandate, macro_context carries veto weight on R/R grounds when the regime is not cleanly risk-on — and it explicitly finds the R/R inadequate. The catalyst_news bullish case is real: earnings May 5 with a lowered bar (soft pre-guidance post Q4 beat), Meta $60B infrastructure deal, TSMC 2nm first-mover, 39 Buy / 0 Sell analyst consensus, and Evercore $358 / Melius $380 targets. However, the stock is already trading near Evercore's bull target ($358), implying much of the good news is priced in. A beat-and-lower Q2 guidance scenario (tariff headwinds on MI325X, Nvidia 89% server GPU share) could trigger a sell-the-news collapse despite a headline earnings beat — a binary risk that inflates the denominator for any pre-earnings entry. Hourly internals across all agents agree: MACD histogram negative (-1.69), hourly -DI (33.53) > +DI (20.45), suggesting intraday distribution is building against an elevated daily price. This is not trend reversal — ADX 73.57 with +DI 56.62 on the daily confirms the macro trend is intact — but it confirms the near-term entry timing is wrong. The productive disagreement between trend_momentum (bullish thesis valid, entry wrong) and mean_reversion (short now on RSI 82 / Z-score 2.47) resolves as: AMD's trend is real and intact, but the stock is statistically overextended and the risk/reward at current price does not justify initiating a new swing position in either direction given the imminent binary earnings event. The correct stance is to wait: for a confirmed breakout above 352.99 with 1.5x+ daily volume (breakout agent's condition), OR for a pullback to the 308-315 zone with hourly MACD re-crossing bullish (trend_momentum and macro_context's condition). Initiating a short (mean_reversion) against ADX 73.57 with earnings 4 days away and no confirmed daily reversal candle is too aggressive. Result: hold/pass until setup clarifies post-earnings or at a lower-risk entry level.
AMD.key_conflicts: The sharpest conflict is between swing_catalyst_news (buy now at 338, earnings catalyst in 4 days, lowered bar = beat likely) and swing_mean_reversion (short now at 337, RSI 82 / Z-score 2.47 = extreme overbought, target reversion to daily EMA-20 at 281). Both agents are looking at the same price and reaching opposite conclusions because they are answering different questions: catalyst_news asks 'is there a fundamental event that justifies the entry?' (yes — earnings); mean_reversion asks 'is price statistically stretched to the point where a snapback outweighs continuation?' (yes — Z-score 2.47, RSI 82). Neither is wrong on its own terms. The second conflict is between the three broadly-bullish agents on entry TIMING: catalyst_news wants to enter pre-earnings at 338; trend_momentum says the trend is real but the entry is premature and wants 308-315; breakout says wait for a confirmed daily close above 352.99 with volume. These three agents are bullish on AMD but cannot agree on where or when to enter — a 3-way timing split that reduces actionability. Macro_context provides the tie-breaking regime check: sub-2:1 R/R at all near-term targets, late-cycle sector rotation (AMD +72% in 21 days vs S&P +9%), and a transitional macro regime (Fed on hold, US-Iran blockade left-tail risk, mega-cap earnings mixed) argue for a pass until the earnings binary resolves or price pulls back to asymmetric levels. The insider distribution signal (CEO Lisa Su $16M in March, 2:1 board sellers vs buyers) adds a modest bearish lean that no agent weighted heavily but that reinforces the 'don't chase' thesis.
AMD.timeframe: wait — re-evaluate post-earnings (May 5) or on pullback to 308-315
AMD.synthesis_notes: 3 bullish / 1 bearish / 1 neutral on direction. However, 2 of the 3 bullish agents (trend_momentum, macro_context which is neutral) explicitly flag premature entry at current levels. Catalyst_news is the only agent advocating immediate entry. Macro_context applies R/R veto (0.5:1 at immediate resistance). Net result: no actionable setup today. The AMD bull thesis remains intact for a lower-risk entry after the earnings binary resolves.
